heading: Return requirements
label: 2662 Return requirements
notes: Effective DateSection applicable to generation-skipping transfers (within the meaning of ) made after , except as otherwise provided, see , set out as a note under .section 2611 of this titlesection 1433 of Pub. L. 99–514section 2601 of this titleOct. 22, 1986

Extension of Time for Filing Return, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–312, title III, § 301(d)(2)124 Stat. 3300Dec. 17, 2010“In the case of any generation-skipping transfer made after , and before the date of the enactment of this Act [], the due date for filing any return under section 2662 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (including any election required to be made on such a return) shall not be earlier than the date which is 9 months after the date of the enactment of this Act.”December 31, 2009Dec. 17, 2010
amendments: Extension of Time for Filing Return, , , provided that:Pub. children.text: the person who is required to make such return shall be the person liable under section 2603(a) for payment of such tax, and

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a direct skip (other than from a trust), on or before the date on which an estate or gift tax return is required to be filed with respect to the transfer, and

children.label: B
children.text: in all other cases, on or before the 15th day of the 4th month after the close of the taxable year of the person required to make such return in which such transfer occurs.

children.heading: Information returns
children.label: b Information returns
children.text: The Secretary may by regulations require a return to be filed containing such information as he determines to be necessary for purposes of this chapter.
